---
title: |
  Materials Genomics<br>Unit 12: Case Studies (Alloys, Polymers, Energy Materials)
bibliography: ref.bib
author:
  - name: Prof. Dr. Philipp Pelz
    affiliation:
      - FAU Erlangen-Nürnberg
execute:
  eval: false
format:
  revealjs:
    width: 1920
    height: 1080
    template-partials:
      - title-slide.html
    css: custom.css
    theme: custom.scss
    slide-number: c/t
    logo: "eclipse_logo_small.png"
    footer: "© Philipp Pelz - Materials Genomics"
---

## 01. Title: Case Studies (Alloys, Polymers, Energy Materials)
- Unit role and synthesis focus.

## 02. Learning outcomes
- Transfer MG workflow to real domains.

## 03. Recap from Units 1–11
- End-to-end toolbox integration.

## 04. Case-study method
- Problem framing and success criteria.

## 05. Case A: alloy design context
- Property targets and constraints.

## 06. Alloy data landscape
- Sources, quality, and coverage.

## 07. Alloy feature engineering
- Composition/process/structure features.

## 08. Alloy model selection
- Baselines vs advanced models.

## 09. Alloy uncertainty handling
- Error bars and confidence-aware ranking.

## 10. Alloy inverse design loop
- Candidate generation and screening.

## 11. Alloy interpretation
- Scientific insight extraction.

## 12. Alloy validation pathway
- Simulation and experiment handoff.

## 13. Case B: polymer informatics context
- Challenges and opportunities.

## 14. Polymer representations
- Fingerprints, graphs, sequence-like encodings.

## 15. Polymer target tasks
- Tg, modulus, stability, processing windows.

## 16. Polymer data challenges
- Small datasets and heterogeneity.

## 17. Polymer model strategies
- Transfer learning and multitask setups.

## 18. Polymer uncertainty and OOD
- Extrapolation guardrails.

## 19. Polymer generative design
- Candidate proposal workflow.

## 20. Polymer practical constraints
- Synthesizability and sustainability.

## 21. Case C: energy materials context
- Batteries, catalysts, photovoltaics.

## 22. Energy-materials objective tradeoffs
- Efficiency, stability, cost.

## 23. Multi-fidelity setup
- Theory + experiment integration.

## 24. Active-learning loop in energy case
- Cost-aware acquisition strategy.

## 25. Physics-informed modeling role
- Constraint and plausibility improvements.

## 26. Candidate prioritization
- Utility and risk balancing.

## 27. Cross-case comparison framework
- What transfers, what is domain-specific.

## 28. Metrics across case studies
- Accuracy, calibration, utility, novelty.

## 29. Reproducibility checklist
- Data lineage and split discipline.

## 30. Communication to domain teams
- Actionable reporting patterns.

## 31. Failure mode #1
- Domain-shift blind spots.

## 32. Failure mode #2
- Leakage through duplicated chemistry families.

## 33. Failure mode #3
- Over-optimizing proxy metrics.

## 34. Mitigation checklist
- Case-study quality control.

## 35. Ethical/sustainability considerations
- Responsible materials discovery.

## 36. Human-in-the-loop practices
- Expert review checkpoints.

## 37. Capstone template
- Reusable project structure.

## 38. Bridge to Unit 13
- Deployment, reproducibility, and MLOps.

## 39. Exercise scaffold: choose one domain
- Define objective + constraints.

## 40. Exercise scaffold: build baseline pipeline
- Data -> model -> evaluation.

## 41. Exercise scaffold: uncertainty + interpretation
- Reliability and insight report.

## 42. Exercise scaffold: design candidate shortlist
- Ranked proposals with rationale.

## 43. Exercise scaffold: validation plan
- Simulation/lab follow-up steps.

## 44. Exercise scaffold: cross-domain reflection
- Transferability analysis.

## 45. Exam-oriented key statements
- High-yield synthesis points.

## 46. Summary table
- Three domains side-by-side.

## 47. Unit summary
- Core takeaways.

## 48. Reading assignment
- MLOps/reproducibility prep.

## 49. References
- Source list.

## 50. Q&A buffer slide
- Discussion and synthesis.

::: {#refs}
:::
